{{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=draft|12=draft}} 公 | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} | Founded = (trad.) {{ill|Kanji 5th Year|ja|寛治5th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (1091) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|October 29|ja|10月29日|12=draft|12=draft}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=雄琴神社|caption=|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} }} [[File:Ichinotorii of Okoto-jinja (Mibu).jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おことじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Tōru-chō, Mibu Town, Shimotsuga District, Tochigi Prefecture. Its former shrine rank is that of a village shrine. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are the following four: * {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=draft|12=draft}} 公 – Ancestor of the Kotsuki Clan and distant ancestor of the Mibu; enshrined together during the Muromachi period. == History == According to the shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanji (寛治5th year , 1091) a descendant of Kiyohara Takenori, Hotei, enshrined his ancestor, {{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王|12=draft|12=draft}}, at this location, marking the foundation of the shrine. It is said that at its inception it was called "Fujimori Jinja" (藤森神社). In the 3rd year of Kanshō (寛正3rd year , 1464), the first head of the Mibu Clan of Shimotsuga District, Mibu Tamenari (壬生胤業), petitioned to enshrine the divine spirit of {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=draft|12=draft}} (distant ancestor of the Mibu and Kotsuki clans) from Ogoto Jinja (in Ogoto, former Ōmi Province, now in Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) and had it enshrined together with the existing deities. At that time, the shrine building was rebuilt and the shrine name was changed to "Ogoto Daimyōjin" (雄琴大明神). The Mibu Clan, supporting the Later Hōjō during the Odawara Campaign in the 18th year of Tenshō (天正18th year , 1590), was defeated; shortly after the surrender of Odawara, the head of the Mibu Clan, Mibu Yoshio, died of illness, leading to the clan’s demise. Even after the fall of the Mibu Clan, Ogoto Jinja continued to be revered as the tutelary and overall guardian shrine of the Mibu. Furthermore, the Mibu family (considered the progenitor of the Mibu Clan as a government family) is said to have made regular pilgrimage to Ogoto Jinja during the 50th anniversary memorial rites at Nikko Tōshō-gū. In the 3rd year of Keiō (慶応3rd year , 1867), a Shinto priest named Kurokawa Yutamaro gathered local priests to form the "Rikama-tai," who served as guides for the New Government forces during the Boshin War. After the Meiji Restoration, under the modern shrine ranking system, the shrine was classified as a village shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okoto-jinja keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shrine Building within the Grounds}}{{small|On the left is the Gate of the Enshrined Deity (Saishinmon), and on the right is the Haiden (Worship Hall).}}]] The main hall is a single-chamber shrine built in the Ryūzō style during the Edo period, constructed in the 3rd year of Jōkyō (貞享3rd year , 1686), with an entrance width of one ken. In addition, the Haiden and the Suishinmon (Gate of the Enshrined Deity) were also built during the Edo period in the Irimoya-zukuri style. These structures (the main hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon) have been designated as tangible cultural properties by Mibu Town. The copper torii at the entrance of the shrine grounds was donated by a prosperous merchant from Mibu in the 7th year of An'ei (安永7th year , 1778) and was cast by the renowned Maruyama family of metalworkers; it has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Tochigi Prefecture. File:Okoto-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) File:Okoto-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Suishinmon (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == The subordinate shrines within the shrine grounds are: * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=draft|12=draft}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=draft|12=draft}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in 1909, the 42nd year of the Meiji era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of individuals associated with the shrine, including members of the Yagi family, as well as those who perished in the First Sino-Japanese War, the Russo-Japanese War, the Second Sino-Japanese War, and the Pacific War. == Rituals == The shrine holds the following annual events: * New Year Festival (January 1) * Don-to Yaki (celebrated on a holiday around January 15) * Setsubun Festival (February 3) * Ininen Festival (March 28) * Cherry Blossom Offering Festival (around the first Sunday in April) * Yasaka Festival (mid-July) * Natsukoshi Ōharai (July 31) * Jūsan'ya Akizuki Festival (on the 13th day of the old lunar 9th month) * Reitaisai (October 29) * Niinamesai and Shinkō (on a Sunday near November 29) == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Property ** Copper Torii (a building shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Mibu Town]] [[Category:History of Mibu Town]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Designated Tangible Cultural Properties in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Fix draft categories]] }} [[sn:Ogoto revision.comment: Text replacement - "|天照大御神|" to "|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|"
revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = [[File:Okoto-jinja torii.JPG|280px]]
Copper Torii (Designated Cultural Property of Tochigi Prefecture) | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shimotsuga District|ja|下都賀郡|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Mibu Town|ja|壬生町|12=draft|12=draft}} Tōru-chō 18-58 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|39.35|N|139|48|22.53|E|region:JP-9_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=draft|12=draft}} 公 | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} | Founded = (trad.) {{ill|Kanji 5th Year|ja|寛治5th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (1091) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|October 29|ja|10月29日|12=draft|12=draft}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=雄琴神社|caption=|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} }} [[File:Ichinotorii of Okoto-jinja (Mibu).jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おことじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Tōru-chō, Mibu Town, Shimotsuga District, Tochigi Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are the following four: * {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=draft|12=draft}} 公 – Ancestor of the Kotsuki Clan and distant ancestor of the Mibu; enshrined together during the Muromachi period. == History == According to the shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanji (寛治5th year , 1091) a descendant of Kiyohara Takenori, Hotei, enshrined his ancestor, {{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王|12=draft|12=draft}}, at this location, marking the foundation of the shrine. It is said that at its inception it was called "Fujimori Jinja" (藤森神社). In the 3rd year of Kanshō (寛正3rd year , 1464), the first head of the Mibu Clan of Shimotsuga District, Mibu Tamenari (壬生胤業), petitioned to enshrine the divine spirit of {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=draft|12=draft}} (distant ancestor of the Mibu and Kotsuki clans) from Ogoto Jinja (in Ogoto, former Ōmi Province, now in Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) and had it enshrined together with the existing deities. At that time, the shrine building was rebuilt and the shrine name was changed to "Ogoto Daimyōjin" (雄琴大明神). The Mibu Clan, supporting the Later Hōjō during the Odawara Campaign in the 18th year of Tenshō (天正18th year , 1590), was defeated; shortly after the surrender of Odawara, the head of the Mibu Clan, Mibu Yoshio, died of illness, leading to the clan’s demise. Even after the fall of the Mibu Clan, Ogoto Jinja continued to be revered as the tutelary and overall guardian shrine of the Mibu. Furthermore, the Mibu family (considered the progenitor of the Mibu Clan as a government family) is said to have made regular pilgrimage to Ogoto Jinja during the 50th anniversary memorial rites at Nikko Tōshō-gū. In the 3rd year of Keiō (慶応3rd year , 1867), a Shinto priest named Kurokawa Yutamaro gathered local priests to form the "Rikama-tai," who served as guides for the New Government forces during the Boshin War. After the Meiji Restoration, under the modern shrine Ranking system, the shrine was classified as a village shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okoto-jinja keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shrine Building within the Grounds}}{{small|On the left is the Gate of the Enshrined Deity (Saishinmon), and on the right is the Haiden (Worship Hall).}}]] The main hall is a single-chamber shrine built in the Ryūzō style during the Edo period, constructed in the 3rd year of Jōkyō (貞享3rd year , 1686), with an entrance width of one ken. In addition, the Haiden and the Suishinmon (Gate of the Enshrined Deity) were also built during the Edo period in the Irimoya-zukuri style. These structures (the main hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon) have been designated as tangible cultural properties by Mibu Town. The copper torii at the entrance of the shrine grounds was donated by a prosperous merchant from Mibu in the 7th year of An'ei (安永7th year , 1778) and was cast by the renowned Maruyama family of metalworkers; it has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Tochigi Prefecture. File:Okoto-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) File:Okoto-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Suishinmon (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == The subordinate shrines within the shrine grounds are: * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=draft|12=draft}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=draft|12=draft}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in 1909, the 42nd year of the Meiji era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of individuals associated with the shrine, including members of the Yagi family, as well as those who perished in the First Sino-Japanese War, the Russo-Japanese War, the Second Sino-Japanese War, and the Pacific War. == Rituals == The shrine holds the following annual events: * New Year Festival (January 1) * Don-to Yaki (celebrated on a holiday around January 15) * Setsubun Festival (February 3) * Ininen Festival (March 28) * Cherry Blossom Offering Festival (around the first Sunday in April) * Yasaka Festival (mid-July) * Natsukoshi Ōharai (July 31) * Jūsan'ya Akizuki Festival (on the 13th day of the old lunar 9th month) * Reitaisai (October 29) * Niinamesai and Shinkō (on a Sunday near November 29) == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Property ** Copper Torii (a building structure) – Designated on August 25, Heisei 1[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2318052.htm Ogoto Jinja Copper Torii] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education “Tochigi Cultural Properties”). === Mibu Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Properties ** Main Hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon (building structures) – Designated on January 1, Heisei 3Shrine grounds explanation board. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shimotsuga District|ja|下都賀郡|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Mibu Town|ja|壬生町|12=draft|12=draft}} Tōru-chō 18-58 '''Access''' * Rail: Tobu Railway, Tobu Utsunomiya Line – Mibu Station (approximately 7 minutes on foot) == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] [[Category:Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Mibu Town]] [[Category:History of Mibu Town]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Designated Tangible Cultural Properties in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Fix draft categories]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Mibu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (壬生町)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (壬生町)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Mibu)}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 雄琴神社 |画像 = [[File:Okoto-jinja torii.JPG|280px]]
銅製鳥居（栃木県指定文化財） |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[下都賀郡]][[壬生町]]通町18-58 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|39.35|N|139|48|22.53|E|region:JP-9_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} |祭神 = [[天照大神|天照大御神]]
[[天武天皇]]
[[舎人親王]]
[[阿保今雄|小槻今雄公]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = （伝）[[寛治]]5年（[[1091年]]） |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月29日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=雄琴神社|caption=|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} }} [[File:Ichinotorii of Okoto-jinja (Mibu).jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|一の鳥居}}]] '''雄琴神社'''（おことじんじゃ）は、[[栃木県]][[下都賀郡]][[壬生町]]通町にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 祭神 == 主祭神は次の4柱。 * [[天照大神|天照皇大神]] * [[天武天皇]] * [[舎人親王]] * [[阿保今雄|小槻今雄]]公 - [[小槻氏]]祖で[[壬生氏]]遠祖。[[室町時代]]の合祀。 == 歴史 == 社伝では、[[寛治]]5年（[[1091年]]）に[[清原武則]]子孫の保定が当地に祖先の[[舎人親王]]を祀ったのが創建とされる。創建当初は「藤森神社」と称したとする。 [[寛正]]3年（[[1464年]]）には、下野[[壬生氏]]初代の[[壬生胤業]]が、[[雄琴神社 (大津市)|雄琴神社]]（[[近江国]]雄琴、現・[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]雄琴）から壬生氏遠祖の[[阿保今雄|小槻今雄]]の神霊を[[勧請]]して合祀したという。その際、社殿を建て替えるとともに社号を「雄琴大明神」に改めたとされる。 壬生氏は、[[天正]]18年（[[1590年]]）の[[小田原征伐]]の際に[[後北条氏]]に味方して敗北し、さらに小田原開城の直後に当主の[[壬生義雄]]が病死したため滅亡した。壬生氏の滅亡以降も、雄琴神社は壬生の総氏神・総鎮守として崇敬を受けた。また、壬生氏の出自とされる[[壬生家 (小槻氏)|壬生家]]（[[官務]]家）は、50年ごとの[[日光東照宮]]年忌法要への関東下向の際、雄琴神社に必ず参拝したと伝わる{{Sfn|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988年}}。 [[慶応]]3年（[[1867年]]）には、神職の黒川豊麿が当地域神職を集めて「利鎌隊」が結成され、[[戊辰戦争]]での新政府軍の案内役を務めている{{Sfn|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988年}}。[[明治維新]]後、[[近代社格制度]]においては[[郷社]]に列した。 == 境内 == [[File:Okoto-jinja keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|境内の社殿}}{{small|左に随神門、右に拝殿。}}]] 本殿は、[[江戸時代]]の[[貞享]]3年（[[1686年]]）の造営で、一間社[[流造]]。また拝殿・随神門も江戸時代の造営で、いずれも[[入母屋造]]である。これら本殿・拝殿・随神門は壬生町指定有形文化財に指定されている。 境内入り口に立つ銅製鳥居は、江戸時代の[[安永]]7年（[[1778年]]）における壬生豪商の加藤作太夫からの寄進になる。当時の代表的な鋳物師である丸山一族の手によるもので、栃木県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 File:Okoto-jinja honden.JPG|本殿（壬生町指定文化財） Haiden of Okoto-jinja (Mibu).jpg|拝殿（壬生町指定文化財） File:Okoto-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|随神門（壬生町指定文化財） == 摂末社 == 拝殿と社務所間を繋ぐ回廊の裏側に鎮座する境内社 Inari-jinja in Okoto--jinja (Mibu).jpg|稲荷神社 Goshaden1 of Okoto-jinja (Mibu).jpg|左の合祀殿（稲荷神社近く） Goshaden2 of Okoto-jinja (Mibu).jpg|右の合祀殿（稲荷神社近く） 社務所の右側に鎮座する境内社 Sanrei-jinja in Okoto-jinja (Mibu).jpg|産霊神社 Itsukushima-jinja in Okoto-jinja (Mibu).jpg|厳島神社 Kotohira-jinja in Okoto-jinja (Mibu).jpg|琴平神社 Ashio-jinja in Okoto-jinja (Mibu).jpg|足尾神社 Yama-jinja in Okoto--jinja (Mibu).jpg|山神社 == 祭事 == * 歳旦祭 （[[1月1日]]） * どんと焼き （[[1月15日]]頃の休日） * 節分祭 （[[2月3日]]） * 祈年祭 （[[3月28日]]） * 献桜祭 （4月第1日曜日頃） * 八坂祭 （7月中旬） * 夏越大祓 （[[7月31日]]） * 十三夜明月祭 （旧暦[[9月13日]]） * 例祭 （[[10月29日]]） * 新嘗祭・御神幸 （[[11月29日]]に近い日曜日） == 文化財 == === 栃木県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 銅製鳥居（建造物） - 平成元年8月25日指定[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2318052.htm 雄琴神社銅製鳥居]（栃木県教育委員会「とちぎの文化財」）。。 === 壬生町指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 本殿・拝殿・随神門（建造物） - 平成3年1月1日指定境内説明板。。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[栃木県]][[下都賀郡]][[壬生町]]通町18-58 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：[[東武鉄道]][[東武宇都宮線|宇都宮線]] [[壬生駅]] （徒歩約7分） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 神社由緒書 * 境内説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編|author=|year=1912|chapter=雄琴神社|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）651-652コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=雄琴神社|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9 栃木県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[雄琴神社 (大津市)|雄琴神社]] （おごとじんじゃ、[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]） - 勧請元。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Okoto-jinja}} * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 雄琴神社] - 栃木県神社庁 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おことしんしや みふ}} [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:栃木県の旧郷社]] [[Category:壬生町の建築物]] [[Category:壬生町の歴史]] [[Category:壬生氏|社おことしんしや みふ]] [[Category:小槻氏|社おことしんしや みふ]] [[Category:栃木県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:栃木県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = [[File:Okoto-jinja torii.JPG|280px]]
Copper Torii (Designated Cultural Property of Tochigi Prefecture) | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}} {{ill|Shimotsuga District|ja|下都賀郡}} {{ill|Mibu Town|ja|壬生町}} Tōru-chō 18-58 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|39.35|N|139|48|22.53|E|region:JP-9_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}
{{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}
{{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王}}
{{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄}} 公 | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Founded = (trad.) {{ill|Kanji 5th Year|ja|寛治5th year}} (1091) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|October 29|ja|10月29日}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=雄琴神社|caption=|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} }} [[File:Ichinotorii of Okoto-jinja (Mibu).jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おことじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Tōru-chō, Mibu Town, Shimotsuga District, Tochigi Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are the following four: * {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神}} * {{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} * {{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王}} * {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄}} 公 – Ancestor of the Kotsuki Clan and distant ancestor of the Mibu; enshrined together during the Muromachi period. == History == According to the shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanji (寛治5th year , 1091) a descendant of Kiyohara Takenori, Hotei, enshrined his ancestor, {{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王}}, at this location, marking the foundation of the shrine. It is said that at its inception it was called "Fujimori Jinja" (藤森神社). In the 3rd year of Kanshō (寛正3rd year , 1464), the first head of the Mibu Clan of Shimotsuga District, Mibu Tamenari (壬生胤業), petitioned to enshrine the divine spirit of {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄}} (distant ancestor of the Mibu and Kotsuki clans) from Ogoto Jinja (in Ogoto, former Ōmi Province, now in Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) and had it enshrined together with the existing deities. At that time, the shrine building was rebuilt and the shrine name was changed to "Ogoto Daimyōjin" (雄琴大明神). The Mibu Clan, supporting the Later Hōjō during the Odawara Campaign in the 18th year of Tenshō (天正18th year , 1590), was defeated; shortly after the surrender of Odawara, the head of the Mibu Clan, Mibu Yoshio, died of illness, leading to the clan’s demise. Even after the fall of the Mibu Clan, Ogoto Jinja continued to be revered as the tutelary and overall guardian shrine of the Mibu. Furthermore, the Mibu family (considered the progenitor of the Mibu Clan as a government family) is said to have made regular pilgrimage to Ogoto Jinja during the 50th anniversary memorial rites at Nikko Tōshō-gū. In the 3rd year of Keiō (慶応3rd year , 1867), a Shinto priest named Kurokawa Yutamaro gathered local priests to form the "Rikama-tai," who served as guides for the New Government forces during the Boshin War. After the Meiji Restoration, under the modern shrine Ranking system, the shrine was classified as a village shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okoto-jinja keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shrine Building within the Grounds}}{{small|On the left is the Gate of the Enshrined Deity (Saishinmon), and on the right is the Haiden (Worship Hall).}}]] The main hall is a single-chamber shrine built in the Ryūzō style during the Edo period, constructed in the 3rd year of Jōkyō (貞享3rd year , 1686), with an entrance width of one ken. In addition, the Haiden and the Suishinmon (Gate of the Enshrined Deity) were also built during the Edo period in the Irimoya-zukuri style. These structures (the main hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon) have been designated as tangible cultural properties by Mibu Town. The copper torii at the entrance of the shrine grounds was donated by a prosperous merchant from Mibu in the 7th year of An'ei (安永7th year , 1778) and was cast by the renowned Maruyama family of metalworkers; it has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Tochigi Prefecture. File:Okoto-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) File:Okoto-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Suishinmon (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == The subordinate shrines within the shrine grounds are: * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in 1909, the 42nd year of the Meiji era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of individuals associated with the shrine, including members of the Yagi family, as well as those who perished in the First Sino-Japanese War, the Russo-Japanese War, the Second Sino-Japanese War, and the Pacific War. == Rituals == The shrine holds the following annual events: * New Year Festival (January 1) * Don-to Yaki (celebrated on a holiday around January 15) * Setsubun Festival (February 3) * Ininen Festival (March 28) * Cherry Blossom Offering Festival (around the first Sunday in April) * Yasaka Festival (mid-July) * Natsukoshi Ōharai (July 31) * Jūsan'ya Akizuki Festival (on the 13th day of the old lunar 9th month) * Reitaisai (October 29) * {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = [[File:Okoto-jinja torii.JPG|280px]]
Copper Torii (Designated Cultural Property of Tochigi Prefecture) | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}} {{ill|Shimotsuga District|ja|下都賀郡}} {{ill|Mibu Town|ja|壬生町}} Tōru-chō 18-58 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|39.35|N|139|48|22.53|E|region:JP-9_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}
{{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}
{{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王}}
{{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄}} 公 | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Founded = (trad.) {{ill|Kanji 5th Year|ja|寛治5th year}} (1091) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|October 29|ja|10月29日}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=雄琴神社|caption=|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} }} [[File:Ichinotorii of Okoto-jinja (Mibu).jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おことじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Tōru-chō, Mibu Town, Shimotsuga District, Tochigi Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are the following four: * {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神}} * {{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} * {{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王}} * {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄}} 公 – Ancestor of the Kotsuki Clan and distant ancestor of the Mibu; enshrined together during the Muromachi period. == History == According to the shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanji (寛治5th year , 1091) a descendant of Kiyohara Takenori, Hotei, enshrined his ancestor, {{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王}}, at this location, marking the foundation of the shrine. It is said that at its inception it was called "Fujimori Jinja" (藤森神社). In the 3rd year of Kanshō (寛正3rd year , 1464), the first head of the Mibu Clan of Shimotsuga District, Mibu Tamenari (壬生胤業), petitioned to enshrine the divine spirit of {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄}} (distant ancestor of the Mibu and Kotsuki clans) from Ogoto Jinja (in Ogoto, former Ōmi Province, now in Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) and had it enshrined together with the existing deities. At that time, the shrine building was rebuilt and the shrine name was changed to "Ogoto Daimyōjin" (雄琴大明神). The Mibu Clan, supporting the Later Hōjō during the Odawara Campaign in the 18th year of Tenshō (天正18th year , 1590), was defeated; shortly after the surrender of Odawara, the head of the Mibu Clan, Mibu Yoshio, died of illness, leading to the clan’s demise. Even after the fall of the Mibu Clan, Ogoto Jinja continued to be revered as the tutelary and overall guardian shrine of the Mibu. Furthermore, the Mibu family (considered the progenitor of the Mibu Clan as a government family) is said to have made regular pilgrimage to Ogoto Jinja during the 50th anniversary memorial rites at Nikko Tōshō-gū. In the 3rd year of Keiō (慶応3rd year , 1867), a Shinto priest named Kurokawa Yutamaro gathered local priests to form the "Rikama-tai," who served as guides for the New Government forces during the Boshin War. After the Meiji Restoration, under the modern shrine Ranking system, the shrine was classified as a village shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okoto-jinja keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shrine Building within the Grounds}}{{small|On the left is the Gate of the Enshrined Deity (Saishinmon), and on the right is the Haiden (Worship Hall).}}]] The main hall is a single-chamber shrine built in the Ryūzō style during the Edo period, constructed in the 3rd year of Jōkyō (貞享3rd year , 1686), with an entrance width of one ken. In addition, the Haiden and the Suishinmon (Gate of the Enshrined Deity) were also built during the Edo period in the Irimoya-zukuri style. These structures (the main hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon) have been designated as tangible cultural properties by Mibu Town. The copper torii at the entrance of the shrine grounds was donated by a prosperous merchant from Mibu in the 7th year of An'ei (安永7th year , 1778) and was cast by the renowned Maruyama family of metalworkers; it has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Tochigi Prefecture. File:Okoto-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) File:Okoto-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Suishinmon (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == The subordinate shrines within the shrine grounds are: * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in 1909, the 42nd year of the Meiji era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of individuals associated with the shrine, including members of the Yagi family, as well as those who perished in the First Sino-Japanese War, the Russo-Japanese War, the Second Sino-Japanese War, and the Pacific War. == Rituals == The shrine holds the following annual events: * New Year Festival (January 1) * Don-to Yaki (celebrated on a holiday around January 15) * Setsubun Festival (February 3) * Ininen Festival (March 28) * Cherry Blossom Offering Festival (around the first Sunday in April) * Yasaka Festival (mid-July) * Natsukoshi Ōharai (July 31) * Jūsan'ya Akizuki Festival (on the 13th day of the old lunar 9th month) * Reitaisai (October 29) * Niinamesai and Shinkō (on a Sunday near November 29) == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Property ** Copper Torii (a building structure) – Designated on August 25, Heisei 1[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2318052.htm Ogoto Jinja Copper Torii] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education “Tochigi Cultural Properties”). === Mibu Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Properties ** Main Hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon (building structures) – Designated on January 1, Heisei 3Shrine In the 3rd year of Kanshō (寛正3rd year , 1464), the first head of the Mibu Clan of Shimotsuga District, Mibu Tamenari (壬生胤業), petitioned to enshrine the divine spirit of {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=draft|12=draft}} (distant ancestor of the Mibu and Kotsuki clans) from Ogoto Jinja (in Ogoto, former Ōmi Province, now in Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) and had it enshrined together with the existing deities. At that time, the shrine building was rebuilt and the shrine name was changed to "Ogoto Daimyōjin" (雄琴大明神). The Mibu Clan, supporting the Later Hōjō during the Odawara Campaign in the 18th year of Tenshō (天正18th year , 1590), was defeated; shortly after the surrender of Odawara, the head of the Mibu Clan, Mibu Yoshio, died of illness, leading to the clan’s demise. Even after the fall of the Mibu Clan, Ogoto Jinja continued to be revered as the tutelary and overall guardian shrine of the Mibu. Furthermore, the Mibu family (considered the progenitor of the Mibu Clan as a government family) is said to have made regular pilgrimage to Ogoto Jinja during the 50th anniversary memorial rites at Nikko Tōshō-gū. In the 3rd year of Keiō (慶応3rd year , 1867), a Shinto priest named Kurokawa Yutamaro gathered local priests to form the "Rikama-tai," who served as guides for the New Government forces during the Boshin War. After the Meiji Restoration, under the modern shrine Ranking system, the shrine was classified as a village shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okoto-jinja keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shrine Building within the Grounds}}{{small|On the left is the Gate of the Enshrined Deity (Saishinmon), and on the right is the Haiden (Worship Hall).}}]] The main hall is a single-chamber shrine built in the Ryūzō style during the Edo period, constructed in the 3rd year of Jōkyō (貞享3rd year , 1686), with an entrance width of one ken. In addition, the Haiden and the Suishinmon (Gate of the Enshrined Deity) were also built during the Edo period in the Irimoya-zukuri style. These structures (the main hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon) have been designated as tangible cultural properties by Mibu Town. The copper torii at the entrance of the shrine grounds was donated by a prosperous merchant from Mibu in the 7th year of An'ei (安永7th year , 1778) and was cast by the renowned Maruyama family of metalworkers; it has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Tochigi Prefecture. File:Okoto-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) File:Okoto-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Suishinmon (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == The subordinate shrines within the shrine grounds are: * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=draft|12=draft}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=draft|12=draft}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in 1909, the 42nd year of the Meiji era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of individuals associated with the shrine, including members of the Yagi family, as well as those who perished in the First Sino-Japanese War, the Russo-Japanese War, the Second Sino-Japanese War, and the Pacific War. == Rituals == The shrine holds the following annual events: * New Year Festival (January 1) * Don-to Yaki (celebrated on a holiday around January 15) * Setsubun Festival (February 3) * Ininen Festival (March 28) * Cherry Blossom Offering Festival (around the first Sunday in April) * Yasaka Festival (mid-July) * Natsukoshi Ōharai (July 31) * Jūsan'ya Akizuki Festival (on the 13th day of the old lunar 9th month) * Reitaisai (October 29) * Niinamesai and Shinkō (on a Sunday near November 29) == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Property ** Copper Torii (a building structure) – Designated on August 25, Heisei 1[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2318052.htm Ogoto Jinja Copper Torii] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education “Tochigi Cultural Properties”). === Mibu Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Properties ** Main Hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon (building structures) – Designated on January 1, Heisei 3Shrine grounds explanation board. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shimotsuga District|ja|下都賀郡|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Mibu {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = [[File:Okoto-jinja torii.JPG|280px]]
Copper Torii (Designated Cultural Property of Tochigi Prefecture) | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shimotsuga District|ja|下都賀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Mibu Town|ja|壬生町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Tōru-chō 18-58 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|39.35|N|139|48|22.53|E|region:JP-9_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 公 | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Founded = (trad.) {{ill|Kanji 5th Year|ja|寛治5th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1091) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|October 29|ja|10月29日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=雄琴神社|caption=|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} }} [[File:Ichinotorii of Okoto-jinja (Mibu).jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おことじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Tōru-chō, Mibu Town, Shimotsuga District, Tochigi Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are the following four: * {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 公 – Ancestor of the Kotsuki Clan and distant ancestor of the Mibu; enshrined together during the Muromachi period. == History == According to the shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanji (寛治5th year , 1091) a descendant of Kiyohara Takenori, Hotei, enshrined his ancestor, {{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, at this location, marking the foundation of the shrine. It is said that at its inception it was called "Fujimori Jinja" (藤森神社). In the 3rd year of Kanshō (寛正3rd year , 1464), the first head of the Mibu Clan of Shimotsuga District, Mibu Tamenari (壬生胤業), petitioned to enshrine the divine spirit of {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (distant ancestor of the Mibu and Kotsuki clans) from Ogoto Jinja (in Ogoto, former Ōmi Province, now in Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) and had it enshrined together with the existing deities. At that time, the shrine building was rebuilt and the shrine name was changed to "Ogoto Daimyōjin" (雄琴大明神). The Mibu Clan, supporting the Later Hōjō during the Odawara Campaign in the 18th year of Tenshō (天正18th year , 1590), was defeated; shortly after the surrender of Odawara, the head of the Mibu Clan, Mibu Yoshio, died of illness, leading to the clan’s demise. Even after the fall of the Mibu Clan, Ogoto Jinja continued to be revered as the tutelary and overall guardian shrine of the Mibu. Furthermore, the Mibu family (considered the progenitor of the Mibu Clan as a government family) is said to have made regular pilgrimage to Ogoto Jinja during the 50th anniversary memorial rites at Nikko Tōshō-gū. In the 3rd year of Keiō (慶応3rd year , 1867), a Shinto priest named Kurokawa Yutamaro gathered local priests to form the "Rikama-tai," who served as guides for the New Government forces during the Boshin War. After the Meiji Restoration, under the modern shrine Ranking system, the shrine was classified as a village shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okoto-jinja keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shrine Building within the Grounds}}{{small|On the left is the Gate of the Enshrined Deity (Saishinmon), and on the right is the Haiden (Worship Hall).}}]] The main hall is a single-chamber shrine built in the Ryūzō style during the Edo period, constructed in the 3rd year of Jōkyō (貞享3rd year , 1686), with an entrance width of one ken. In addition, the Haiden and the Suishinmon (Gate of the Enshrined Deity) were also built during the Edo period in the Irimoya-zukuri style. These structures (the main hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon) have been designated as tangible cultural properties by Mibu Town. The copper torii at the entrance of the shrine grounds was donated by a prosperous merchant from Mibu in the 7th year of An'ei (安永7th year , 1778) and was cast by the renowned Maruyama family of metalworkers; it has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Tochigi Prefecture. File:Okoto-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) File:Okoto-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Suishinmon (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == The subordinate shrines within the shrine grounds are: * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in 1909, the 42nd year of the Meiji era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of individuals associated with the shrine, including members of the Yagi family, as well as those who perished in the First Sino-Japanese War, the Russo-Japanese War, the Second Sino-Japanese War, and the Pacific War. == Rituals == The shrine holds the following annual events: * New Year Festival (January 1) * Don-to Yaki (celebrated on a holiday around January 15) * Setsubun Festival (February 3) * Ininen Festival (March 28) * Cherry Blossom Offering Festival (around the first Sunday in April) * Yasaka Festival (mid-July) * Natsukoshi Ōharai (July 31) * Jūsan'ya Akizuki Festival (on the 13th day of the old lunar 9th month) * Reitaisai (October 29) * Niinamesai and Shinkō (on a Sunday near November 29) == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Property ** Copper Torii (a building structure) – Designated on August 25, Heisei 1[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2318052.htm Ogoto Jinja Copper Torii] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education “Tochigi Cultural Properties”). === Mibu Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Properties ** Main Hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon (building structures) – Designated on January 1, Heisei 3Shrine grounds explanation board. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shimotsuga District|ja|下都賀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Mibu Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are the following four: * {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 公 – Ancestor of the Kotsuki Clan and distant ancestor of the Mibu; enshrined together during the Muromachi period. == History == According to the shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanji (寛治5th year , 1091) a descendant of Kiyohara Takenori, Hotei, enshrined his ancestor, {{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, at this location, marking the foundation of the shrine. It is said that at its inception it was called "Fujimori Jinja" (藤森神社). In the 3rd year of Kanshō (寛正3rd year , 1464), the first head of the Mibu Clan of Shimotsuga District, Mibu Tamenari (壬生胤業), petitioned to enshrine the divine spirit of {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (distant ancestor of the Mibu and Kotsuki clans) from Ogoto Jinja (in Ogoto, former Ōmi Province, now in Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) and had it enshrined together with the existing deities. At that time, the shrine building was rebuilt and the shrine name was changed to "Ogoto Daimyōjin" (雄琴大明神). The Mibu Clan, supporting the Later Hōjō during the Odawara Campaign in the 18th year of Tenshō (天正18th year , 1590), was defeated; shortly after the surrender of Odawara, the head of the Mibu Clan, Mibu Yoshio, died of illness, leading to the clan’s demise. Even after the fall of the Mibu Clan, Ogoto Jinja continued to be revered as the tutelary and overall guardian shrine of the Mibu. Furthermore, the Mibu family (considered the progenitor of the Mibu Clan as a government family) is said to have made regular pilgrimage to Ogoto Jinja during the 50th anniversary memorial rites at Nikko Tōshō-gū. In the 3rd year of Keiō (慶応3rd year , 1867), a Shinto priest named Kurokawa Yutamaro gathered local priests to form the "Rikama-tai," who served as guides for the New Government forces during the Boshin War. After the Meiji Restoration, under the modern shrine Ranking system, the shrine was classified as a village shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okoto-jinja keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shrine Building within the Grounds}}{{small|On the left is the Gate of the Enshrined Deity (Saishinmon), and on the right is the Haiden (Worship Hall).}}]] The main hall is a single-chamber shrine built in the Ryūzō style during the Edo period, constructed in the 3rd year of Jōkyō (貞享3rd year , 1686), with an entrance width of one ken. In addition, the Haiden and the Suishinmon (Gate of the Enshrined Deity) were also built during the Edo period in the Irimoya-zukuri style. These structures (the main hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon) have been designated as tangible cultural properties by Mibu Town. The copper torii at the entrance of the shrine grounds was donated by a prosperous merchant from Mibu in the 7th year of An'ei (安永7th year , 1778) and was cast by the renowned Maruyama family of metalworkers; it has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Tochigi Prefecture. File:Okoto-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) File:Okoto-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Suishinmon (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == The subordinate shrines within the shrine grounds are: * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in 1909, the 42nd year of the Meiji era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of individuals associated with the shrine, including members of the Yagi family, as well as those who perished in the First Sino-Japanese War, the Russo-Japanese War, the Second Sino-Japanese War, and the Pacific War. == Rituals == The shrine holds the following annual events: * New Year Festival (January 1) * Don-to Yaki (celebrated on a holiday around January 15) * Setsubun Festival (February 3) * Ininen Festival (March 28) * Cherry Blossom Offering Festival (around the first Sunday in April) * Yasaka Festival (mid-July) * Natsukoshi Ōharai (July 31) * Jūsan'ya Akizuki Festival (on the 13th day of the old lunar 9th month) * Reitaisai (October 29) * Niinamesai and Shinkō (on a Sunday near November 29) == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Property ** Copper Torii (a building structure) – Designated on August 25, Heisei 1[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2318052.htm Ogoto Jinja Copper Torii] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education “Tochigi Cultural Properties”). === Mibu Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Properties ** Main Hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon (building structures) – Designated on January 1, Heisei 3Shrine grounds explanation board. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shimotsuga District|ja|下都賀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Mibu Town|ja|壬生町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Tōru-chō 18-58 '''Access''' * Rail: Tobu Railway, Tobu Utsunomiya Line – Mibu Station (approximately 7 minutes on foot) == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * File:Okoto-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) File:Okoto-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Suishinmon (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == The subordinate shrines within the shrine grounds are: * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in 1909, the 42nd year of the Meiji era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of individuals associated with the shrine, including members of the Yagi family, as well as those who perished in the First Sino-Japanese War, the Russo-Japanese War, the Second Sino-Japanese War, and the Pacific War. == Rituals == The shrine holds the following annual events: * New Year Festival (January 1) * Don-to Yaki (celebrated on a holiday around January 15) * Setsubun Festival (February 3) * Ininen Festival (March 28) * Cherry Blossom Offering Festival (around the first Sunday in April) * Yasaka Festival (mid-July) * Natsukoshi Ōharai (July 31) * Jūsan'ya Akizuki Festival (on the 13th day of the old lunar 9th month) * Reitaisai (October 29) * Niinamesai and Shinkō (on a Sunday near November 29) == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Property ** Copper Torii (a building structure) – Designated on August 25, Heisei 1[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2318052.htm Ogoto Jinja Copper Torii] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education “Tochigi Cultural Properties”). === Mibu Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Properties ** Main Hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon (building structures) – Designated on January 1, Heisei 3Shrine grounds explanation board. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shimotsuga District|ja|下都賀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Mibu Town|ja|壬生町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Tōru-chō 18-58 '''Access''' * Rail: Tobu Railway, Tobu Utsunomiya Line – Mibu Station (approximately 7 minutes on foot) == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] [[Category:Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Mibu Town]] [[Category:History of Mibu Town]] revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = [[File:Okoto-jinja torii.JPG|280px]]
Copper Torii (Designated Cultural Property of Tochigi Prefecture) | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shimotsuga District|ja|下都賀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Mibu Town|ja|壬生町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Tōru-chō 18-58 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|39.35|N|139|48|22.53|E|region:JP-9_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 公 | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Founded = (trad.) {{ill|Kanji 5th Year|ja|寛治5th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1091) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|October 29|ja|10月29日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=雄琴神社|caption=|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} }} [[File:Ichinotorii of Okoto-jinja (Mibu).jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おことじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Tōru-chō, Mibu Town, Shimotsuga District, Tochigi Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are the following four: * {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 公 – Ancestor of the Kotsuki Clan and distant ancestor of the Mibu; enshrined together during the Muromachi period. == History == According to the shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanji (寛治5th year , 1091) a descendant of Kiyohara Takenori, Hotei, enshrined his ancestor, {{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, at this location, marking the foundation of the shrine. It is said that at its inception it was called "Fujimori Jinja" (藤森神社). In the 3rd year of Kanshō (寛正3rd year , 1464), the first head of the Mibu Clan of Shimotsuga District, Mibu Tamenari (壬生胤業), petitioned to enshrine the divine spirit of {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (distant ancestor of the Mibu and Kotsuki clans) from Ogoto Jinja (in Ogoto, former Ōmi Province, now in Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) and had it enshrined together with the existing deities. At that time, the shrine building was rebuilt and the shrine name was changed to "Ogoto Daimyōjin" (雄琴大明神). The Mibu Clan, supporting the Later Hōjō during the Odawara Campaign in the 18th year of Tenshō (天正18th year , 1590), was defeated; shortly after the surrender of Odawara, the head of the Mibu Clan, Mibu Yoshio, died of illness, leading to the clan’s demise. Even after the fall of the Mibu Clan, Ogoto Jinja continued to be revered as the tutelary and overall guardian shrine of the Mibu. Furthermore, the Mibu family (considered the progenitor of the Mibu Clan as a government family) is said to have made regular pilgrimage to Ogoto Jinja during the 50th anniversary memorial rites at Nikko Tōshō-gū. In the 3rd year of Keiō (慶応3rd year , 1867), a Shinto priest named Kurokawa Yutamaro gathered local priests to form the "Rikama-tai," who served as guides for the New Government forces during the Boshin War. After the Meiji Restoration, under the modern shrine Ranking system, the shrine was classified as a village shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okoto-jinja keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shrine Building within the Grounds}}{{small|On the left is the Gate of the Enshrined Deity (Saishinmon), and on the right is the Haiden (Worship Hall).}}]] The main hall is a single-chamber shrine built in the Ryūzō style during the Edo period, constructed in the 3rd year of Jōkyō (貞享3rd year , 1686), with an entrance width of one ken. In addition, the Haiden and the Suishinmon (Gate of the Enshrined Deity) were also built during the Edo period in the Irimoya-zukuri style. These structures (the main hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon) have been designated as tangible cultural properties by Mibu Town. The copper torii at the entrance of the shrine grounds was donated by a prosperous merchant from Mibu in the 7th year of An'ei (安永7th year , 1778) and was cast by the renowned Maruyama family of metalworkers; it has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Tochigi Prefecture. File:Okoto-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) File:Okoto-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Suishinmon (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == The subordinate shrines within the shrine grounds are: * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in 1909, the 42nd year of the Meiji era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of individuals associated with the shrine, including members of the Yagi family, as well as those who perished in the First Sino-Japanese War, the Russo-Japanese War, the Second Sino-Japanese War, and the Pacific War. == Rituals == The shrine holds the following annual events: * New Year Festival (January 1) * Don-to Yaki (celebrated on a holiday around January 15) * Setsubun Festival (February 3) * Ininen Festival (March 28) * Cherry Blossom Offering Festival (around the first Sunday in April) * Yasaka Festival (mid-July) * Natsukoshi Ōharai (July 31) * Jūsan'ya Akizuki Festival (on the 13th day of the old lunar 9th month) * Reitaisai (October 29) * Niinamesai and Shinkō (on a Sunday near November 29) == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Property ** Copper Torii (a building structure) – Designated on August 25, Heisei 1[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2318052.htm Ogoto Jinja Copper Torii] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education “Tochigi Cultural Properties”). === Mibu Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Properties ** Main Hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon (building structures) – Designated on January 1, Heisei 3Shrine grounds explanation board. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shimotsuga District|ja|下都賀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Mibu Town|ja|壬生町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Tōru-chō 18-58 '''Access''' * Rail: Tobu Railway, Tobu Utsunomiya Line – Mibu Station (approximately 7 minutes on foot) == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|雄琴神社 (壬生町)|version=97187318|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] [[Category:Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Mibu Town]] [[Category:History of Mibu Town]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Designated Tangible Cultural Properties in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:Fix draft categories]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Mibu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (壬生町)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (壬生町)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = [[File:Okoto-jinja torii.JPG|280px]]
Copper Torii (Designated Cultural Property of Tochigi Prefecture) | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Shimotsuga District|ja|下都賀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuga District|qq=}} {{ill|Mibu Town|ja|壬生町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu Town|qq=}} Tōru-chō 18-58 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|39.35|N|139|48|22.53|E|region:JP-9_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}
{{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}
{{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shanen-shinō|qq=}}
{{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|qq=}} 公 | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | Founded = (trad.) {{ill|Kanji 5th Year|ja|寛治5th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanji 5th Year|qq=}} (1091) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūzō|qq=}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|October 29|ja|10月29日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 29|qq=}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=雄琴神社|caption=|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} }} [[File:Ichinotorii of Okoto-jinja (Mibu).jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おことじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Tōru-chō, Mibu Town, Shimotsuga District, Tochigi Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are the following four: * {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}} * {{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} * {{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shanen-shinō|qq=}} * {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|qq=}} 公 – Ancestor of the Kotsuki Clan and distant ancestor of the Mibu; enshrined together during the Muromachi period. == History == According to the shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanji (寛治5th year , 1091) a descendant of Kiyohara Takenori, Hotei, enshrined his ancestor, {{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shanen-shinō|qq=}}, at this location, marking the foundation of the shrine. It is said that at its inception it was called "Fujimori Jinja" (藤森神社). In the 3rd year of Kanshō (寛正3rd year , 1464), the first head of the Mibu Clan of Shimotsuga District, Mibu Tamenari (壬生胤業), petitioned to enshrine the divine spirit of {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|qq=}} (distant ancestor of the Mibu and Kotsuki clans) from Ogoto Jinja (in Ogoto, former Ōmi Province, now in Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) and had it enshrined together with the existing deities. At that time, the shrine building was rebuilt and the shrine name was changed to "Ogoto Daimyōjin" (雄琴大明神). The Mibu Clan, supporting the Later Hōjō during the Odawara Campaign in the 18th year of Tenshō (天正18th year , 1590), was defeated; shortly after the surrender of Odawara, the head of the Mibu Clan, Mibu Yoshio, died of illness, leading to the clan’s demise. Even after the fall of the Mibu Clan, Ogoto Jinja continued to be revered as the tutelary and overall guardian shrine of the Mibu. Furthermore, the Mibu family (considered the progenitor of the Mibu Clan as a government family) is said to have made regular pilgrimage to Ogoto Jinja during the 50th anniversary memorial rites at Nikko Tōshō-gū. In the 3rd year of Keiō (慶応3rd year , 1867), a Shinto priest named Kurokawa Yutamaro gathered local priests to form the "Rikama-tai," who served as guides for the New Government forces during the Boshin War. After the Meiji Restoration, under the modern shrine Ranking system, the shrine was classified as a village shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okoto-jinja keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shrine Building within the Grounds}}{{small|On the left is the Gate of the Enshrined Deity (Saishinmon), and on the right is the Haiden (Worship Hall).}}]] The main hall is a single-chamber shrine built in the Ryūzō style during the Edo period, constructed in the 3rd year of Jōkyō (貞享3rd year , 1686), with an entrance width of one ken. In addition, the Haiden and the Suishinmon (Gate of the Enshrined Deity) were also built during the Edo period in the Irimoya-zukuri style. These structures (the main hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon) have been designated as tangible cultural properties by Mibu Town. The copper torii at the entrance of the shrine grounds was donated by a prosperous merchant from Mibu in the 7th year of An'ei (安永7th year , 1778) and was cast by the renowned Maruyama family of metalworkers; it has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Tochigi Prefecture. File:Okoto-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) File:Okoto-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Suishinmon (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == The subordinate shrines within the shrine grounds are: * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|qq=}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in 1909, the 42nd year of the Meiji era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of individuals associated with the shrine, including members of the Yagi family, as well as those who perished in the First Sino-Japanese War, the Russo-Japanese War, the Second Sino-Japanese War, and the Pacific War. == Rituals == The shrine holds the following annual events: * New Year Festival (January 1) * Don-to Yaki (celebrated on a holiday around January 15) * Setsubun Festival (February 3) * Ininen Festival (March 28) * Cherry Blossom Offering Festival (around the first Sunday in April) * Yasaka Festival (mid-July) * Natsukoshi Ōharai (July 31) * Jūsan'ya Akizuki Festival (on the 13th day of the old lunar 9th month) * Reitaisai (October 29) * Niinamesai and Shinkō (on a Sunday near November 29) == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Property ** Copper Torii (a building structure) – Designated on August 25, Heisei 1[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2318052.htm Ogoto Jinja Copper Torii] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education “Tochigi Cultural Properties”). === Mibu Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Properties ** Main Hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon (building structures) – Designated on January 1, Heisei 3Shrine grounds explanation board. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Shimotsuga District|ja|下都賀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuga District|qq=}} {{ill|Mibu Town|ja|壬生町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu Town|qq=}} Tōru-chō 18-58 '''Access''' * Rail: Tobu Railway, Tobu Utsunomiya Line – Mibu Station (approximately 7 minutes on foot) == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja {{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shanen-shinō|qq=}}
{{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|qq=}} 公 | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | Founded = (trad.) {{ill|Kanji 5th Year|ja|寛治5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanji 5th Year|qq=}} (1091) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūzō|qq=}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|October 29|ja|10月29日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 29|qq=}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=雄琴神社|caption=|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} }} [[File:Ichinotorii of Okoto-jinja (Mibu).jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おことじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Tōru-chō, Mibu Town, Shimotsuga District, Tochigi Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are the following four: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}} * {{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} * {{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shanen-shinō|qq=}} * {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|qq=}} 公 – Ancestor of the Kotsuki Clan and distant ancestor of the Mibu; enshrined together during the Muromachi period. == History == According to the shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanji (寛治5th year , 1091) a descendant of Kiyohara Takenori, Hotei, enshrined his ancestor, {{ill|Shanen-shinō|ja|舎人親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shanen-shinō|qq=}}, at this location, marking the foundation of the shrine. It is said that at its inception it was called "Fujimori Jinja" (藤森神社). In the 3rd year of Kanshō (寛正3rd year , 1464), the first head of the Mibu Clan of Shimotsuga District, Mibu Tamenari (壬生胤業), petitioned to enshrine the divine spirit of {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|qq=}} (distant ancestor of the Mibu and Kotsuki clans) from Ogoto Jinja (in Ogoto, former Ōmi Province, now in Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) and had it enshrined together with the existing deities. At that time, the shrine building was rebuilt and the shrine name was changed to "Ogoto Daimyōjin" (雄琴大明神). The Mibu Clan, supporting the Later Hōjō during the Odawara Campaign in the 18th year of Tenshō (天正18th year , 1590), was defeated; shortly after the surrender of Odawara, the head of the Mibu Clan, Mibu Yoshio, died of illness, leading to the clan’s demise. Even after the fall of the Mibu Clan, Ogoto Jinja continued to be revered as the tutelary and overall guardian shrine of the Mibu. Furthermore, the Mibu family (considered the progenitor of the Mibu Clan as a government family) is said to have made regular pilgrimage to Ogoto Jinja during the 50th anniversary memorial rites at Nikko Tōshō-gū. In the 3rd year of Keiō (慶応3rd year , 1867), a Shinto priest named Kurokawa Yutamaro gathered local priests to form the "Rikama-tai," who served as guides for the New Government forces during the Boshin War. After the Meiji Restoration, under the modern shrine Ranking system, the shrine was classified as a village shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okoto-jinja keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shrine Building within the Grounds}}{{small|On the left is the Gate of the Enshrined Deity (Saishinmon), and on the right is the Haiden (Worship Hall).}}]] The main hall is a single-chamber shrine built in the Ryūzō style during the Edo period, constructed in the 3rd year of Jōkyō (貞享3rd year , 1686), with an entrance width of one ken. In addition, the Haiden and the Suishinmon (Gate of the Enshrined Deity) were also built during the Edo period in the Irimoya-zukuri style. These structures (the main hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon) have been designated as tangible cultural properties by Mibu Town. The copper torii at the entrance of the shrine grounds was donated by a prosperous merchant from Mibu in the 7th year of An'ei (安永7th year , 1778) and was cast by the renowned Maruyama family of metalworkers; it has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Tochigi Prefecture. File:Okoto-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) File:Okoto-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Suishinmon (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == The subordinate shrines within the shrine grounds are: * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|qq=}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in 1909, the 42nd year of the Meiji era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of individuals associated with the shrine, including members of the Yagi family, as well as those who perished in the First Sino-Japanese War, the Russo-Japanese War, the Second Sino-Japanese War, and the Pacific War. == Rituals == The shrine holds the following annual events: * New Year Festival (January 1) * Don-to Yaki (celebrated on a holiday around January 15) * Setsubun Festival (February 3) * Ininen Festival (March 28) * Cherry Blossom Offering Festival (around the first Sunday in April) * Yasaka Festival (mid-July) * Natsukoshi Ōharai (July 31) * Jūsan'ya Akizuki Festival (on the 13th day of the old lunar 9th month) * Reitaisai (October 29) * Niinamesai and Shinkō (on a Sunday near November 29) == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Property ** Copper Torii (a building structure) – Designated on August 25, Heisei 1[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2318052.htm Ogoto Jinja Copper Torii] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education “Tochigi Cultural Properties”). === Mibu Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Properties ** Main Hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon (building structures) – Designated on January 1, Heisei 3Shrine grounds explanation board. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Shimotsuga District|ja|下都賀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuga District|qq=}} {{ill|Mibu Town|ja|壬生町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu Town|qq=}} Tōru-chō 18-58 '''Access''' * Rail: Tobu Railway, Tobu Utsunomiya Line – Mibu Station (approximately 7 minutes on foot) == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|雄琴神社 (壬生町)|version=97187318|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] [[Category:Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Mibu Town]] [[Category:History of Mibu Town]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Designated Tangible Cultural Properties in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:Fix draft categories]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Mibu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (壬生町)]]
