[[sn:commons:Category:Okoto-jinja]][[ja:雄琴神社 (壬生町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11659232]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = File:Okoto-jinja torii.JPG | Location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]] [[:en:Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]] [[:en:Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu Town]] Tōru-chō 18-58 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|39.35|N|139|48|22.53|E|region:JP-9_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}
{{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}
[[:en:Prince Toneri|Shanen-shinō]]
{{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿保今雄|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} 公 | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Founded = (trad.) {{ill|Kanji 5th Year|ja|寛治5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanji 5th Year|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1091) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = [[:en:October 29|October 29]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=雄琴神社|caption=|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Mibu|WD=Q1354910}}; {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.42761111111111|N|139.80625|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}} }} [[File:Ichinotorii of Okoto-jinja (Mibu).jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おことじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Tōru-chō, Mibu Town, Shimotsuga District, Tochigi Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are the following four: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} * [[:en:Prince Toneri|Shanen-shinō]] * {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿保今雄|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} 公 – Ancestor of the Kotsuki Clan and distant ancestor of the Mibu; enshrined together during the Muromachi period. == History == According to the shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanji (寛治5th year , 1091) a descendant of Kiyohara Takenori, Hotei, enshrined his ancestor, [[:en:Prince Toneri|Shanen-shinō]], at this location, marking the foundation of the shrine. It is said that at its inception it was called "Fujimori Jinja" (藤森神社). In the 3rd year of Kanshō (寛正3rd year , 1464), the first head of the Mibu Clan of Shimotsuga District, Mibu Tamenari (壬生胤業), petitioned to enshrine the divine spirit of {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿保今雄|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} (distant ancestor of the Mibu and Kotsuki clans) from Ogoto Jinja (in Ogoto, former Ōmi Province, now in Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) and had it enshrined together with the existing deities. At that time, the shrine building was rebuilt and the shrine name was changed to "Ogoto Daimyōjin" (雄琴大明神). The Mibu Clan, supporting the Later Hōjō during the Odawara Campaign in the 18th year of Tenshō (天正18th year , 1590), was defeated; shortly after the surrender of Odawara, the head of the Mibu Clan, Mibu Yoshio, died of illness, leading to the clan’s demise. Even after the fall of the Mibu Clan, Ogoto Jinja continued to be revered as the tutelary and overall guardian shrine of the Mibu. Furthermore, the Mibu family (considered the progenitor of the Mibu Clan as a government family) is said to have made regular pilgrimage to Ogoto Jinja during the 50th anniversary memorial rites at Nikko Tōshō-gū. In the 3rd year of Keiō (慶応3rd year , 1867), a Shinto priest named Kurokawa Yutamaro gathered local priests to form the "Rikama-tai," who served as guides for the New Government forces during the Boshin War. After the Meiji Restoration, under the modern shrine Ranking system, the shrine was classified as a village shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okoto-jinja keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shrine Building within the Grounds}}{{small|On the left is the Gate of the Enshrined Deity (Saishinmon), and on the right is the Haiden (Worship Hall).}}]] The main hall is a single-chamber shrine built in the Ryūzō style during the Edo period, constructed in the 3rd year of Jōkyō (貞享3rd year , 1686), with an entrance width of one ken. In addition, the Haiden and the Suishinmon (Gate of the Enshrined Deity) were also built during the Edo period in the Irimoya-zukuri style. These structures (the main hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon) have been designated as tangible cultural properties by Mibu Town. The copper torii at the entrance of the shrine grounds was donated by a prosperous merchant from Mibu in the 7th year of An'ei (安永7th year , 1778) and was cast by the renowned Maruyama family of metalworkers; it has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Tochigi Prefecture. File:Okoto-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) File:Okoto-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Suishinmon (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == The subordinate shrines within the shrine grounds are: * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami]], {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in 1909, the 42nd year of the Meiji era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of individuals associated with the shrine, including members of the Yagi family, as well as those who perished in the First Sino-Japanese War, the Russo-Japanese War, the Second Sino-Japanese War, and the Pacific War. == Rituals == The shrine holds the following annual events: * New Year Festival (January 1) * Don-to Yaki (celebrated on a holiday around January 15) * Setsubun Festival (February 3) * Ininen Festival (March 28) * Cherry Blossom Offering Festival (around the first Sunday in April) * Yasaka Festival (mid-July) * Natsukoshi Ōharai (July 31) * Jūsan'ya Akizuki Festival (on the 13th day of the old lunar 9th month) * Reitaisai (October 29) * Niinamesai and Shinkō (on a Sunday near November 29) == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Property ** Copper Torii (a building structure) – Designated on August 25, Heisei 1[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2318052.htm Ogoto Jinja Copper Torii] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education “Tochigi Cultural Properties”). === Mibu Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Properties ** Main Hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon (building structures) – Designated on January 1, Heisei 3Shrine grounds explanation board. == Local Information == '''Location''' * [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]] [[:en:Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]] [[:en:Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu Town]] Tōru-chō 18-58 '''Access''' * Rail: Tobu Railway, Tobu Utsunomiya Line – Mibu Station (approximately 7 minutes on foot) == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical [[sn:commons:Category:Okoto-jinja]][[ja:雄琴神社 (壬生町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11659232]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = File:Okoto-jinja torii.JPG | Location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]] [[:en:Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]] [[:en:Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu Town]] Tōru-chō 18-58 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|39.35|N|139|48|22.53|E|region:JP-9_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}
{{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}
[[:en:Prince Toneri|Shanen-shinō]]
{{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿保今雄|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} 公 | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Founded = (trad.) {{ill|Kanji 5th Year|ja|寛治5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanji 5th Year|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1091) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = [[:en:October 29|October 29]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=雄琴神社|caption=|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Mibu|WD=Q1354910}}; {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.42761111111111|N|139.80625|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}} }} [[File:Ichinotorii of Okoto-jinja (Mibu).jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おことじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Tōru-chō, Mibu Town, Shimotsuga District, Tochigi Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are the following four: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} * [[:en:Prince Toneri|Shanen-shinō]] * {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿保今雄|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} 公 – Ancestor of the Kotsuki Clan and distant ancestor of the Mibu; enshrined together during the Muromachi period. == History == According to the shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanji (寛治5th year , 1091) a descendant of Kiyohara Takenori, Hotei, enshrined his ancestor, [[:en:Prince Toneri|Shanen-shinō]], at this location, marking the foundation of the shrine. It is said that at its inception it was called "Fujimori Jinja" (藤森神社). In the 3rd year of Kanshō (寛正3rd year , 1464), the first head of the Mibu Clan of Shimotsuga District, Mibu Tamenari (壬生胤業), petitioned to enshrine the divine spirit of {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿保今雄|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} (distant ancestor of the Mibu and Kotsuki clans) from Ogoto Jinja (in Ogoto, former Ōmi Province, now in Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) and had it enshrined together with the existing deities. At that time, the shrine building was rebuilt and the shrine name was changed to "Ogoto Daimyōjin" (雄琴大明神). The Mibu Clan, supporting the Later Hōjō during the Odawara Campaign in the 18th year of Tenshō (天正18th year , 1590), was defeated; shortly after the surrender of Odawara, the head of the Mibu Clan, Mibu Yoshio, died of illness, leading to the clan’s demise. Even after the fall of the Mibu Clan, Ogoto Jinja continued to be revered as the tutelary and overall guardian shrine of the Mibu. Furthermore, the Mibu family (considered the progenitor of the Mibu Clan as a government family) is said to have made regular pilgrimage to Ogoto Jinja during the 50th anniversary memorial rites at Nikko Tōshō-gū. In the 3rd year of Keiō (慶応3rd year , 1867), a Shinto priest named Kurokawa Yutamaro gathered local priests to form the "Rikama-tai," who served as guides for the New Government forces during the Boshin War. After the Meiji Restoration, under the modern shrine Ranking system, the shrine was classified as a village shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okoto-jinja keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shrine Building within the Grounds}}{{small|On the left is the Gate of the Enshrined Deity (Saishinmon), and on the right is the Haiden (Worship Hall).}}]] The main hall is a single-chamber shrine built in the Ryūzō style during the Edo period, constructed in the 3rd year of Jōkyō (貞享3rd year , 1686), with an entrance width of one ken. In addition, the Haiden and the Suishinmon (Gate of the Enshrined Deity) were also built during the Edo period in the Irimoya-zukuri style. These structures (the main hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon) have been designated as tangible cultural properties by Mibu Town. The copper torii at the entrance of the shrine grounds was donated by a prosperous merchant from Mibu in the 7th year of An'ei (安永7th year , 1778) and was cast by the renowned Maruyama family of metalworkers; it has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Tochigi Prefecture. File:Okoto-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) File:Okoto-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Suishinmon (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == The subordinate shrines within the shrine grounds are: * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami]], {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in 1909, the 42nd year of the Meiji era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of individuals associated with the shrine, including members of the Yagi family, as well as those who perished in the First Sino-Japanese War, the Russo-Japanese War, the Second Sino-Japanese War, and the Pacific War. == Rituals == The shrine holds the following annual events: * New Year Festival (January 1) * Don-to Yaki (celebrated on a holiday around January 15) * Setsubun Festival (February 3) * Ininen Festival (March 28) * Cherry Blossom Offering Festival (around the first Sunday in April) * Yasaka Festival (mid-July) * Natsukoshi Ōharai (July 31) * Jūsan'ya Akizuki Festival (on the 13th day of the old lunar 9th month) * Reitaisai (October 29) * Niinamesai and Shinkō (on a Sunday near November 29) == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Property ** Copper Torii (a building structure) – Designated on August 25, Heisei 1[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2318052.htm Ogoto Jinja Copper Torii] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education “Tochigi Cultural Properties”). === Mibu Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Properties ** Main Hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon (building structures) – Designated on January 1, Heisei 3Shrine grounds explanation board. == Local Information == '''Location''' * [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]] [[:en:Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]] [[:en:Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu Town]] Tōru-chō 18-58 '''Access''' * Rail: Tobu Railway, Tobu Utsunomiya Line – Mibu Station (approximately 7 minutes on foot) == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|雄琴神社 (壬生町)|version=97187318|comment=Imported full ja history}} File:Okoto-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) File:Okoto-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Suishinmon (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == The subordinate shrines within the shrine grounds are: * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami]], {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in 1909, the 42nd year of the Meiji era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of individuals associated with the shrine, including members of the Yagi family, as well as those who perished in the First Sino-Japanese War, the Russo-Japanese War, the Second Sino-Japanese War, and the Pacific War. == Rituals == The shrine holds the following annual events: * New Year Festival (January 1) * Don-to Yaki (celebrated on a holiday around January 15) * Setsubun Festival (February 3) * Ininen Festival (March 28) * Cherry Blossom Offering Festival (around the first Sunday in April) * Yasaka Festival (mid-July) * Natsukoshi Ōharai (July 31) * Jūsan'ya Akizuki Festival (on the 13th day of the old lunar 9th month) * Reitaisai (October 29) * Niinamesai and Shinkō (on a Sunday near November 29) == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Property ** Copper Torii (a building structure) – Designated on August 25, Heisei 1[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2318052.htm Ogoto Jinja Copper Torii] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education “Tochigi Cultural Properties”). === Mibu Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Properties ** Main Hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon (building structures) – Designated on January 1, Heisei 3Shrine grounds explanation board. == Local Information == '''Location''' * [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]] [[:en:Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]] [[:en:Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu Town]] Tōru-chō 18-58 '''Access''' * Rail: Tobu Railway, Tobu Utsunomiya Line – Mibu Station (approximately 7 minutes on foot) == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [[sn:commons:Category:Okoto-jinja]][[ja:雄琴神社 (壬生町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11659232]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = File:Okoto-jinja torii.JPG | Location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]] [[:en:Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]] [[:en:Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu Town]] Tōru-chō 18-58 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|39.35|N|139|48|22.53|E|region:JP-9_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}
{{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}
[[:en:Prince Toneri|Shanen-shinō]]
{{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿保今雄|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} 公 | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Founded = (trad.) {{ill|Kanji 5th Year|ja|寛治5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanji 5th Year|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1091) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = [[:en:October 29|October 29]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=雄琴神社|caption=|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Mibu|WD=Q1354910}}; {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.42761111111111|N|139.80625|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}} }} [[File:Ichinotorii of Okoto-jinja (Mibu).jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おことじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Tōru-chō, Mibu Town, Shimotsuga District, Tochigi Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are the following four: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} * [[:en:Prince Toneri|Shanen-shinō]] * {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿保今雄|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} 公 – Ancestor of the Kotsuki Clan and distant ancestor of the Mibu; enshrined together during the Muromachi period. == History == According to the shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanji (寛治5th year , 1091) a descendant of Kiyohara Takenori, Hotei, enshrined his ancestor, [[:en:Prince Toneri|Shanen-shinō]], at this location, marking the foundation of the shrine. It is said that at its inception it was called "Fujimori Jinja" (藤森神社). In the 3rd year of Kanshō (寛正3rd year , 1464), the first head of the Mibu Clan of Shimotsuga District, Mibu Tamenari (壬生胤業), petitioned to enshrine the divine spirit of {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿保今雄|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} (distant ancestor of the Mibu and Kotsuki clans) from Ogoto Jinja (in Ogoto, former Ōmi Province, now in Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) and had it enshrined together with the existing deities. At that time, the shrine building was rebuilt and the shrine name was changed to "Ogoto Daimyōjin" (雄琴大明神). The Mibu Clan, supporting the Later Hōjō during the Odawara Campaign in the 18th year of Tenshō (天正18th year , 1590), was defeated; shortly after the surrender of Odawara, the head of the Mibu Clan, Mibu Yoshio, died of illness, leading to the clan’s demise. Even after the fall of the Mibu Clan, Ogoto Jinja continued to be revered as the tutelary and overall guardian shrine of the Mibu. Furthermore, the Mibu family (considered the progenitor of the Mibu Clan as a government family) is said to have made regular pilgrimage to Ogoto Jinja during the 50th anniversary memorial rites at Nikko Tōshō-gū. In the 3rd year of Keiō (慶応3rd year , 1867), a Shinto priest named Kurokawa Yutamaro gathered local priests to form the "Rikama-tai," who served as guides for the New Government forces during the Boshin War. After the Meiji Restoration, under the modern shrine Ranking system, the shrine was classified as a village shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okoto-jinja keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shrine Building within the Grounds}}{{small|On the left is the Gate of the Enshrined Deity (Saishinmon), and on the right is the Haiden (Worship Hall).}}]] The main hall is a single-chamber shrine built in the Ryūzō style during the Edo period, constructed in the 3rd year of Jōkyō (貞享3rd year , 1686), with an entrance width of one ken. In addition, the Haiden and the Suishinmon (Gate of the Enshrined Deity) were also built during the Edo period in the Irimoya-zukuri style. These structures (the main hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon) have been designated as tangible cultural properties by Mibu Town. The copper torii at the entrance of the shrine grounds was donated by a prosperous merchant from Mibu in the 7th year of An'ei (安永7th year , 1778) and was cast by the renowned Maruyama family of metalworkers; it has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Tochigi Prefecture. File:Okoto-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) File:Okoto-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Suishinmon (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == The subordinate shrines within the shrine grounds are: * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami]], {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in 1909, the 42nd year of the Meiji era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of individuals associated with the shrine, including members of the Yagi family, as well as those who perished in the First Sino-Japanese War, the Russo-Japanese War, the Second Sino-Japanese War, and the Pacific War. == Rituals == The shrine holds the following annual events: * New Year Festival (January 1) * Don-to Yaki (celebrated on a holiday around January 15) * Setsubun Festival (February 3) * Ininen Festival (March 28) * Cherry Blossom Offering Festival (around the first Sunday in April) * Yasaka Festival (mid-July) * Natsukoshi Ōharai (July 31) * Jūsan'ya Akizuki Festival (on the 13th day of the old lunar 9th month) * Reitaisai (October 29) * Niinamesai and Shinkō (on a Sunday near November 29) == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Property ** Copper Torii (a building structure) – Designated on August 25, Heisei 1[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2318052.htm Ogoto Jinja Copper Torii] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education “Tochigi Cultural Properties”). === Mibu Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Properties ** Main Hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon (building structures) – Designated on January 1, Heisei 3Shrine grounds explanation board. == Local Information == '''Location''' * [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]] [[:en:Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]] [[:en:Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu Town]] Tōru-chō 18-58 '''Access''' * Rail: Tobu Railway, Tobu Utsunomiya Line – Mibu Station (approximately 7 minutes on foot) == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|雄琴神社 (壬生町)|version=97187318|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Son-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Mibu Town]] [[Category:History of Mibu Town]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Tochigi Prefecture]] Furthermore, the Mibu family (considered the progenitor of the Mibu Clan as a government family) is said to have made regular pilgrimage to Ogoto Jinja during the 50th anniversary memorial rites at Nikko Tōshō-gū. In the 3rd year of Keiō (慶応3rd year , 1867), a Shinto priest named Kurokawa Yutamaro gathered local priests to form the "Rikama-tai," who served as guides for the New Government forces during the Boshin War. After the Meiji Restoration, under the modern shrine Ranking system, the shrine was classified as a village shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okoto-jinja keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shrine Building within the Grounds}}{{small|On the left is the Gate of the Enshrined Deity (Saishinmon), and on the right is the Haiden (Worship Hall).}}]] The main hall is a single-chamber shrine built in the Ryūzō style during the Edo period, constructed in the 3rd year of Jōkyō (貞享3rd year , 1686), with an entrance width of one ken. In addition, the Haiden and the Suishinmon (Gate of the Enshrined Deity) were also built during the Edo period in the Irimoya-zukuri style. These structures (the main hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon) have been designated as tangible cultural properties by Mibu Town. The copper torii at the entrance of the shrine grounds was donated by a prosperous merchant from Mibu in the 7th year of An'ei (安永7th year , 1778) and was cast by the renowned Maruyama family of metalworkers; it has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Tochigi Prefecture. File:Okoto-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) File:Okoto-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Suishinmon (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == The subordinate shrines within the shrine grounds are: * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami]], {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in 1909, the 42nd year of the Meiji era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of individuals associated with the shrine, including members of the Yagi family, as well as those who perished in the First Sino-Japanese War, the Russo-Japanese War, the Second Sino-Japanese War, and the Pacific War. == Rituals == The shrine holds the following annual events: * New Year Festival (January 1) * Don-to Yaki (celebrated on a holiday around January 15) * Setsubun Festival (February 3) * Ininen Festival (March 28) * Cherry Blossom Offering Festival (around the first Sunday in April) * Yasaka Festival (mid-July) * Natsukoshi Ōharai (July 31) * Jūsan'ya Akizuki Festival (on the 13th day of the old lunar 9th month) * Reitaisai (October 29) * Niinamesai and Shinkō (on a Sunday near November 29) == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Property ** Copper Torii (a building [[sn:commons:Category:Okoto-jinja]][[ja:雄琴神社 (壬生町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11659232]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = File:Okoto-jinja torii.JPG | Location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]] [[:en:Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]] [[:en:Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu Town]] Tōru-chō 18-58 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|39.35|N|139|48|22.53|E|region:JP-9_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}
{{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}
[[:en:Prince Toneri|Shanen-shinō]]
{{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿保今雄|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} 公 | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Founded = (trad.) {{ill|Kanji 5th Year|ja|寛治5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanji 5th Year|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1091) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = [[:en:October 29|October 29]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=雄琴神社|caption=|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Mibu|WD=Q1354910}}; {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.42761111111111|N|139.80625|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}} }} [[File:Ichinotorii of Okoto-jinja (Mibu).jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おことじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Tōru-chō, Mibu Town, Shimotsuga District, Tochigi Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are the following four: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} * [[:en:Prince Toneri|Shanen-shinō]] * {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿保今雄|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} 公 – Ancestor of the Kotsuki Clan and distant ancestor of the Mibu; enshrined together during the Muromachi period. == History == According to the shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanji (寛治5th year , 1091) a descendant of Kiyohara Takenori, Hotei, enshrined his ancestor, [[:en:Prince Toneri|Shanen-shinō]], at this location, marking the foundation of the shrine. It is said that at its inception it was called "Fujimori Jinja" (藤森神社). In the 3rd year of Kanshō (寛正3rd year , 1464), the first head of the Mibu Clan of Shimotsuga District, Mibu Tamenari (壬生胤業), petitioned to enshrine the divine spirit of {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿保今雄|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} (distant ancestor of the Mibu and Kotsuki clans) from Ogoto Jinja (in Ogoto, former Ōmi Province, now in Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) and had it enshrined together with the existing deities. At that time, the shrine building was rebuilt and the shrine name was changed to "Ogoto Daimyōjin" (雄琴大明神). The Mibu Clan, supporting the Later Hōjō during the Odawara Campaign in the 18th year of Tenshō (天正18th year , 1590), was defeated; shortly after the surrender of Odawara, the head of the Mibu Clan, Mibu Yoshio, died of illness, leading to the clan’s demise. Even after the fall of the Mibu Clan, Ogoto Jinja continued to be revered as the tutelary and overall guardian shrine of the Mibu. Furthermore, the Mibu family (considered the progenitor of the Mibu Clan as a government family) is said to have made regular pilgrimage to Ogoto Jinja during the 50th anniversary memorial rites at Nikko Tōshō-gū. In the 3rd year of Keiō (慶応3rd year , 1867), a Shinto priest named Kurokawa Yutamaro gathered local priests to form the "Rikama-tai," who served as guides for the New Government forces during the Boshin War. After the Meiji Restoration, under the modern shrine Ranking system, the shrine was classified as a village shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okoto-jinja keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shrine Building within the Grounds}}{{small|On the left is the Gate of the Enshrined Deity (Saishinmon), and on the right is the Haiden (Worship Hall).}}]] The main hall is a single-chamber shrine built in the Ryūzō style during the Edo period, constructed in the 3rd year of Jōkyō (貞享3rd year , 1686), with an entrance width of one ken. In addition, the Haiden and the Suishinmon (Gate of the Enshrined Deity) were also built during the Edo period in the Irimoya-zukuri style. These structures (the main hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon) have been designated as tangible cultural properties by Mibu Town. The copper torii at the entrance of the shrine grounds was donated by a prosperous merchant from Mibu in the 7th year of An'ei (安永7th year , 1778) and was cast by the renowned Maruyama family of metalworkers; it has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Tochigi Prefecture. File:Okoto-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) File:Okoto-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Suishinmon (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == The subordinate shrines within the shrine grounds are: * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami]], {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in 1909, the 42nd year of the Meiji era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of individuals associated with the shrine, including members of the Yagi family, as well as those who perished in the First Sino-Japanese War, the Russo-Japanese War, the Second Sino-Japanese War, and the Pacific War. == Rituals == The shrine holds the following annual events: * New Year Festival (January 1) * Don-to Yaki (celebrated on a holiday around January 15) * Setsubun Festival (February 3) * Ininen Festival (March 28) * Cherry Blossom Offering Festival (around the first Sunday in April) * Yasaka Festival (mid-July) * Natsukoshi Ōharai (July 31) * Jūsan'ya Akizuki Festival (on the 13th day of the old lunar 9th month) * Reitaisai (October 29) * Niinamesai and Shinkō (on a Sunday near November 29) == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Property ** Copper Torii (a building Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are the following four: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} * [[:en:Prince Toneri|Shanen-shinō]] * {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿保今雄|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} 公 – Ancestor of the Kotsuki Clan and distant ancestor of the Mibu; enshrined together during the Muromachi period. == History == According to the shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanji (寛治5th year , 1091) a descendant of Kiyohara Takenori, Hotei, enshrined his ancestor, [[:en:Prince Toneri|Shanen-shinō]], at this location, marking the foundation of the shrine. It is said that at its inception it was called "Fujimori Jinja" (藤森神社). In the 3rd year of Kanshō (寛正3rd year , 1464), the first head of the Mibu Clan of Shimotsuga District, Mibu Tamenari (壬生胤業), petitioned to enshrine the divine spirit of {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿保今雄|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} (distant ancestor of the Mibu and Kotsuki clans) from Ogoto Jinja (in Ogoto, former Ōmi Province, now in Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) and had it enshrined together with the existing deities. At that time, the shrine building was rebuilt and the shrine name was changed to "Ogoto Daimyōjin" (雄琴大明神). The Mibu Clan, supporting the Later Hōjō during the Odawara Campaign in the 18th year of Tenshō (天正18th year , 1590), was defeated; shortly after the surrender of Odawara, the head of the Mibu Clan, Mibu Yoshio, died of illness, leading to the clan’s demise. Even after the fall of the Mibu Clan, Ogoto Jinja continued to be revered as the tutelary and overall guardian shrine of the Mibu. Furthermore, the Mibu family (considered the progenitor of the Mibu Clan as a government family) is said to have made regular pilgrimage to Ogoto Jinja during the 50th anniversary memorial rites at Nikko Tōshō-gū. In the 3rd year of Keiō (慶応3rd year , 1867), a Shinto priest named Kurokawa Yutamaro gathered local priests to form the "Rikama-tai," who served as guides for the New Government forces during the Boshin War. After the Meiji Restoration, under the modern shrine Ranking system, the shrine was classified as a village shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okoto-jinja keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shrine Building within the Grounds}}{{small|On the left is the Gate of the Enshrined Deity (Saishinmon), and on the right is the Haiden (Worship Hall).}}]] The main hall is a single-chamber shrine built in the Ryūzō style during the Edo period, constructed in the 3rd year of Jōkyō (貞享3rd year , 1686), with an entrance width of one ken. In addition, the Haiden and the Suishinmon (Gate of the Enshrined Deity) were also built during the Edo period in the Irimoya-zukuri style. These structures (the main hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon) have been designated as tangible cultural properties by Mibu Town. The copper torii at the entrance of the shrine grounds was donated by a prosperous merchant from Mibu in the 7th year of An'ei (安永7th year , 1778) and was cast by the renowned Maruyama family of metalworkers; it has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Tochigi Prefecture. File:Okoto-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) File:Okoto-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Suishinmon (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == The subordinate shrines within the shrine grounds are: * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami]], {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in 1909, the 42nd year of the Meiji era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of individuals associated with the shrine, including members of the Yagi family, as well as those who perished in the First Sino-Japanese War, the Russo-Japanese War, the Second Sino-Japanese War, and the Pacific War. == Rituals == The shrine holds the following annual events: * New Year Festival (January 1) * Don-to Yaki (celebrated on a holiday around January 15) * Setsubun Festival (February 3) * Ininen Festival (March 28) * Cherry Blossom Offering Festival (around the first Sunday in April) * Yasaka Festival (mid-July) * Natsukoshi Ōharai (July 31) * Jūsan'ya Akizuki Festival (on the 13th day of the old lunar 9th month) * Reitaisai (October 29) * Niinamesai and Shinkō (on a Sunday near November 29) == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Property ** Copper Torii (a building structure) – Designated on August 25, Heisei 1[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2318052.htm Ogoto Jinja Copper Torii] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education “Tochigi Cultural Properties”). === Mibu Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Properties ** Main Hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon (building structures) – Designated on January 1, Heisei 3Shrine grounds explanation board. == Local Information == '''Location''' * [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]] [[:en:Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]] [[:en:Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu Town]] Tōru-chō 18-58 '''Access''' * Rail: Tobu Railway, Tobu Utsunomiya Line – Mibu Station (approximately 7 minutes on foot) == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are the following four: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} * [[:en:Prince Toneri|Shanen-shinō]] * {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿保今雄|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} 公 – Ancestor of the Kotsuki Clan and distant ancestor of the Mibu; enshrined together during the Muromachi period. == History == According to the shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanji (寛治5th year , 1091) a descendant of Kiyohara Takenori, Hotei, enshrined his ancestor, [[:en:Prince Toneri|Shanen-shinō]], at this location, marking the foundation of the shrine. At that time, the shrine building was rebuilt and the shrine name was changed to "Ogoto Daimyōjin" (雄琴大明神). The Mibu Clan, supporting the Later Hōjō during the Odawara Campaign in the 18th year of Tenshō (天正18th year , 1590), was defeated; shortly after the surrender of Odawara, the head of the Mibu Clan, Mibu Yoshio, died of illness, leading to the clan’s demise. Furthermore, the Mibu family (considered the progenitor of the Mibu Clan as a government family) is said to have made regular pilgrimage to Ogoto Jinja during the 50th anniversary memorial rites at Nikko Tōshō-gū. In the 3rd year of Keiō (慶応3rd year , 1867), a Shinto priest named Kurokawa Yutamaro gathered local priests to form the "Rikama-tai," who served as guides for the New Government forces during the Boshin War. After the Meiji Restoration, under the modern shrine Ranking system, the shrine was classified as a village shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okoto-jinja keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shrine Building within the Grounds}}{{small|On the left is the Gate of the Enshrined Deity (Saishinmon), and on the right is the Haiden (Worship Hall).}}]] The main hall is a single-chamber shrine built in the Ryūzō style during the Edo period, constructed in the 3rd year of Jōkyō (貞享3rd year , 1686), with an entrance width of one ken. The copper torii at the entrance of the shrine grounds was donated by a prosperous merchant from Mibu in the 7th year of An'ei (安永7th year , 1778) and was cast by the renowned Maruyama family of metalworkers; it has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Tochigi Prefecture. File:Okoto-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) File:Okoto-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Suishinmon (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == The subordinate shrines within the shrine grounds are: * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami]], {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}. [[:en:Prince Toneri|Shanen-shinō]]
{{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿保今雄|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} 公 | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Founded = (trad.) {{ill|Kanji 5th Year|ja|寛治5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanji 5th Year|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1091) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = [[:en:October 29|October 29]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=雄琴神社|caption=|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Mibu|WD=Q1354910}}; {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.42761111111111|N|139.80625|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}} }} [[File:Ichinotorii of Okoto-jinja (Mibu).jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おことじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Tōru-chō, Mibu Town, Shimotsuga District, Tochigi Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are the following four: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} * [[:en:Prince Toneri|Shanen-shinō]] * {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿保今雄|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} 公 – Ancestor of the Kotsuki Clan and distant ancestor of the Mibu; enshrined together during the Muromachi period. == History == According to the shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanji (寛治5th year , 1091) a descendant of Kiyohara Takenori, Hotei, enshrined his ancestor, [[:en:Prince Toneri|Shanen-shinō]], at this location, marking the foundation of the shrine. It is said that at its inception it was called "Fujimori Jinja" (藤森神社). In the 3rd year of Kanshō (寛正3rd year , 1464), the first head of the Mibu Clan of Shimotsuga District, Mibu Tamenari (壬生胤業), petitioned to enshrine the divine spirit of {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿保今雄|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} (distant ancestor of the Mibu and Kotsuki clans) from Ogoto Jinja (in Ogoto, former Ōmi Province, now in Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) and had it enshrined together with the existing deities. At that time, the shrine building was rebuilt and the shrine name was changed to "Ogoto Daimyōjin" (雄琴大明神). The Mibu Clan, supporting the Later Hōjō during the Odawara Campaign in the 18th year of Tenshō (天正18th year , 1590), was defeated; shortly after the surrender of Odawara, the head of the Mibu Clan, Mibu Yoshio, died of illness, leading to the clan’s demise. Even after the fall of the Mibu Clan, Ogoto Jinja continued to be revered as the tutelary and overall guardian shrine of the Mibu. Furthermore, the Mibu family (considered the progenitor of the Mibu Clan as a government family) is said to have made regular pilgrimage to Ogoto Jinja during the 50th anniversary memorial rites at Nikko Tōshō-gū. In the 3rd year of Keiō (慶応3rd year , 1867), a Shinto priest named Kurokawa Yutamaro gathered local priests to form the "Rikama-tai," who served as guides for the New Government forces during the Boshin War. After the Meiji Restoration, under the modern shrine Ranking system, the shrine was classified as a village shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okoto-jinja keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shrine Building within the Grounds}}{{small|On the left is the Gate of the Enshrined Deity (Saishinmon), and on the right is the Haiden (Worship Hall).}}]] The main hall is a single-chamber shrine built in the Ryūzō style during the Edo period, constructed in the 3rd year of Jōkyō (貞享3rd year , 1686), with an entrance width of one ken. In addition, the Haiden and the Suishinmon (Gate of the Enshrined Deity) were also built during the Edo period in the Irimoya-zukuri style. These structures (the main hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon) have been designated as tangible cultural properties by Mibu Town. The copper torii at the entrance of the shrine grounds was donated by a prosperous merchant from Mibu in the 7th year of An'ei (安永7th year , 1778) and was cast by the renowned Maruyama family of metalworkers; it has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Tochigi Prefecture. File:Okoto-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) File:Okoto-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Suishinmon (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == The subordinate shrines within the shrine grounds are: * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami]], {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in 1909, the 42nd year of the Meiji era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of individuals associated with the shrine, including members of the Yagi family, as well as those who perished in the First Sino-Japanese War, the Russo-Japanese War, the Second Sino-Japanese War, and the Pacific War. == Rituals == The shrine holds the following annual events: * New Year Festival (January 1) * Don-to Yaki (celebrated on a holiday around January 15) * Setsubun Festival (February 3) * Ininen Festival (March 28) * Cherry Blossom Offering Festival (around the first Sunday in April) * Yasaka Festival (mid-July) * Natsukoshi Ōharai (July 31) * Jūsan'ya Akizuki Festival (on the 13th day of the old lunar 9th month) * Reitaisai (October 29) * Niinamesai and Shinkō (on a Sunday near November 29) == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Property ** Copper Torii (a building structure) – Designated on August 25, Heisei 1[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2318052.htm Ogoto Jinja Copper Torii] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education “Tochigi Cultural Properties”). === Mibu Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Properties ** Main Hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon (building structures) – Designated on January 1, Heisei 3Shrine grounds explanation board. == Local Information == '''Location''' * [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]] [[:en:Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]] [[:en:Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu [[commons:Category:Okoto-jinja]][[ja:雄琴神社 (壬生町)]] [[commons:Category:Okoto-jinja]][[ja:雄琴神社 (壬生町)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Okoto-jinja]][[ja:雄琴神社 (壬生町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11659232]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = File:Okoto-jinja torii.JPG | Location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]] [[:en:Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]] [[:en:Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu Town]] Tōru-chō 18-58 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|39.35|N|139|48|22.53|E|region:JP-9_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}
{{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}
[[:en:Prince Toneri|Shanen-shinō]]
{{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿保今雄|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} 公 | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Founded = (trad.) {{ill|Kanji 5th Year|ja|寛治5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanji 5th Year|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1091) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = [[:en:October 29|October 29]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=雄琴神社|caption=|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Mibu|WD=Q1354910}}; {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.42761111111111|N|139.80625|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}} }} [[File:Ichinotorii of Okoto-jinja (Mibu).jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|First Torii}}]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おことじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Tōru-chō, Mibu Town, Shimotsuga District, Tochigi Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are the following four: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} * [[:en:Prince Toneri|Shanen-shinō]] * {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿保今雄|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} 公 – Ancestor of the Kotsuki Clan and distant ancestor of the Mibu; enshrined together during the Muromachi period. == History == According to the shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanji (寛治5th year , 1091) a descendant of Kiyohara Takenori, Hotei, enshrined his ancestor, [[:en:Prince Toneri|Shanen-shinō]], at this location, marking the foundation of the shrine. It is said that at its inception it was called "Fujimori Jinja" (藤森神社). In the 3rd year of Kanshō (寛正3rd year , 1464), the first head of the Mibu Clan of Shimotsuga District, Mibu Tamenari (壬生胤業), petitioned to enshrine the divine spirit of {{ill|Kotsuki Imao|ja|小槻今雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Imao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿保今雄|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} (distant ancestor of the Mibu and Kotsuki clans) from Ogoto Jinja (in Ogoto, former Ōmi Province, now in Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) and had it enshrined together with the existing deities. At that time, the shrine building was rebuilt and the shrine name was changed to "Ogoto Daimyōjin" (雄琴大明神). The Mibu Clan, supporting the Later Hōjō during the Odawara Campaign in the 18th year of Tenshō (天正18th year , 1590), was defeated; shortly after the surrender of Odawara, the head of the Mibu Clan, Mibu Yoshio, died of illness, leading to the clan’s demise. Even after the fall of the Mibu Clan, Ogoto Jinja continued to be revered as the tutelary and overall guardian shrine of the Mibu. Furthermore, the Mibu family (considered the progenitor of the Mibu Clan as a government family) is said to have made regular pilgrimage to Ogoto Jinja during the 50th anniversary memorial rites at Nikko Tōshō-gū. In the 3rd year of Keiō (慶応3rd year , 1867), a Shinto priest named Kurokawa Yutamaro gathered local priests to form the "Rikama-tai," who served as guides for the New Government forces during the Boshin War. After the Meiji Restoration, under the modern shrine Ranking system, the shrine was classified as a village shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okoto-jinja keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shrine Building within the Grounds}}{{small|On the left is the Gate of the Enshrined Deity (Saishinmon), and on the right is the Haiden (Worship Hall).}}]] The main hall is a single-chamber shrine built in the Ryūzō style during the Edo period, constructed in the 3rd year of Jōkyō (貞享3rd year , 1686), with an entrance width of one ken. In addition, the Haiden and the Suishinmon (Gate of the Enshrined Deity) were also built during the Edo period in the Irimoya-zukuri style. These structures (the main hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon) have been designated as tangible cultural properties by Mibu Town. The copper torii at the entrance of the shrine grounds was donated by a prosperous merchant from Mibu in the 7th year of An'ei (安永7th year , 1778) and was cast by the renowned Maruyama family of metalworkers; it has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Tochigi Prefecture. File:Okoto-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) File:Okoto-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Suishinmon (Designated tangible cultural property by Mibu Town) == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == The subordinate shrines within the shrine grounds are: * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami]], {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in 1909, the 42nd year of the Meiji era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of individuals associated with the shrine, including members of the Yagi family, as well as those who perished in the First Sino-Japanese War, the Russo-Japanese War, the Second Sino-Japanese War, and the Pacific War. == Rituals == The shrine holds the following annual events: * New Year Festival (January 1) * Don-to Yaki (celebrated on a holiday around January 15) * Setsubun Festival (February 3) * Ininen Festival (March 28) * Cherry Blossom Offering Festival (around the first Sunday in April) * Yasaka Festival (mid-July) * Natsukoshi Ōharai (July 31) * Jūsan'ya Akizuki Festival (on the 13th day of the old lunar 9th month) * Reitaisai (October 29) * Niinamesai and Shinkō (on a Sunday near November 29) == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Property ** Copper Torii (a building structure) – Designated on August 25, Heisei 1[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2318052.htm Ogoto Jinja Copper Torii] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education “Tochigi Cultural Properties”). === Mibu Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Properties ** Main Hall, Haiden, and Suishinmon (building structures) – Designated on January 1, Heisei 3Shrine grounds explanation board. == Local Information == '''Location''' * [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]] [[:en:Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]] [[:en:Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu Town]] Tōru-chō 18-58 '''Access''' * Rail: Tobu Railway, Tobu Utsunomiya Line – Mibu Station (approximately 7 minutes on foot) == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|雄琴神社 (壬生町)|version=97187318|comment=Imported full ja history}}
